creator: tphilipp@potion-studios.com
text:
Unfortunately, still happening with latest ports tree (as of 10 minutes ago), however I'm still on FreeBSD 9.3, but unlikely that that would be related? As you can see below, showconfig has it toggled on, but configure doesn't enable it:

  "Building with these protocols: purple"

As said, forcing --jabber=1 to configure does work.


$ uname -v
FreeBSD 9.3-RELEASE-p41 #0 r239187M: Tue May 17 12:40:29 CEST 2016     root@boite:/usr/obj/encrypted/usr/src/sys/VT
$
$ make showconfig
===> The following configuration options are available for bitlbee-3.4.2_1:
     DEBUG=off: Build with debugging support
     DOCS=on: Build and/or install documentation
     EXAMPLES=on: Build and/or install examples
     OTR=on: Off-the-Record Messaging support
====> Options available for the multi PROTO: you have to choose at least one of them
     JABBER=on: Jabber communications protocol support
     LIBPURPLE=on: Use libpurple for protocol support
     MSN=off: Microsoft Messenger support
     OSCAR=off: OSCAR (ICQ, AIM) support
     SKYPE=off: Skype support
     TWITTER=off: Twitter support
     YAHOO=off: Yahoo! Messenger support
====> Options available for the single EVENTS: you have to select exactly one of them
     GLIB=on: Use glib as event handler
     LIBEVENT=off: Use libevent as event handler
====> SSL protocol support: you have to select exactly one of them
     OPENSSL=on: SSL/TLS support via OpenSSL
     GNUTLS=off: SSL/TLS support via GnuTLS
     NSS=off: SSL/TLS support via NSS
===> Use 'make config' to modify these settings
$
$ make configure
===>  License GPLv2+ accepted by the user
===>  Found saved configuration for bitlbee-3.4.2_1
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on file: /usr/local/sbin/pkg - found
===> Fetching all distfiles required by bitlbee-3.4.2_1 for building
===>  Extracting for bitlbee-3.4.2_1
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for bitlbee-3.4.2.tar.gz.
===>  Patching for bitlbee-3.4.2_1
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on executable: xmlto - found
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on executable: xsltproc - found
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on executable: gmake - found
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on package: pkgconf>=0.9.10 - found
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on file: /usr/local/bin/python2.7 - found
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on shared library: libotr.so.5 - found (/usr/local/lib/libotr.so.5)
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on shared library: libpurple.so - found (/usr/local/lib/libpurple.so)
===>   bitlbee-3.4.2_1 depends on shared library: libglib-2.0.so - found (/usr/local/lib/libglib-2.0.so)
===>  Configuring for bitlbee-3.4.2_1
BitlBee configure

No detection code exists for OpenSSL. Make sure that you have a complete
installation of OpenSSL (including devel/header files) before reporting
compilation problems.

Also, keep in mind that the OpenSSL is, according to some people, not
completely GPL-compatible. Using GnuTLS is recommended and better supported
by us. However, on many BSD machines, OpenSSL can be considered part of the
operating system, which makes it GPL-compatible.

For more info, see: http://www.openssl.org/support/faq.html#LEGAL2
                    http://www.gnome.org/~markmc/openssl-and-the-gpl.html

Please note that distributing a BitlBee binary which links to OpenSSL is
probably illegal. If you want to create and distribute a binary BitlBee
package, you really should use GnuTLS instead.

Also, the OpenSSL license requires us to say this:
 *    "This product includes software developed by the OpenSSL Project
 *    for use in the OpenSSL Toolkit. (http://www.openssl.org/)"
WARNING: Your version of make (BSD make?) does not support BitlBee's makefiles.
BitlBee needs GNU make to build properly. On most systems GNU make is available
under the name 'gmake'.

gmake seems to be available on your machine, great.

Architecture: FreeBSD

Configuration done:
  Debugging disabled.
  AddressSanitizer (ASAN) disabled.
  Building PIE executable
  Binary stripping enabled.
  Off-the-Record (OTR) Messaging enabled.
  systemd disabled.
  Using event handler: glib
  Using SSL library: openssl
  Building with these protocols: purple
    Note that BitlBee-libpurple is supported on a best-effort basis. It's
    not *fully* compatible with normal BitlBee. Don't use it unless you
    absolutely need it (i.e. support for a certain protocol or feature).
